pages.full_text:
delicious!
__Saturday__
We slept well & it was a bit chilly in the morning so we breakfasted in the van. Then Naomi took Thomas to Banbury Station to go back to play a concert at St. Augustines. She returned & we had coffee before setting off for __Stowe__.
We walked near the Elysian field which was very beautiful. Lots of temples & pillars, & views. It was built by Lord Cobham, 1760? Lovely big trees & places to picnic. Sold to NT by school in 1989.
pages.notes: Page number not visible in top-left corner of this left-hand page. The text 'delicious!' at the top is a continuation from the previous page. 'St. Augustines' could refer to St Augustine's church/venue. 'Lord Cobham, 1760?' — the diarist adds a question mark themselves. 'Sold to NT by school in 1989' refers to the National Trust acquisition of Stowe. One glued-in photo of Stowe gardens (colonnade/temple building). Year cannot be firmly established from this page alone; context suggests this is a weekend trip sometime during the volume's 2003–2019 range.

pages.full_text:
We walked, had a picnic at the van & walked again & had a cup of tea. V. large fish, geese.

We were very lucky with the weather. As we drove from the carpark (Naomi to visit Keisha in Suffolk, Simon & Eve home) to the lodge, it started to rain again. It was a very good weekend, not least because Naomi taught us what kissing gates are for.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 55 visible in top-right corner. Two glued-in photos of Stowe garden buildings — one showing a columned temple/Palladian building, one showing a rotunda/temple reflected in a lake. The text 'kissing gates are for' appears to continue or be cut off at the right margin but the sense is complete as a knowing remark. 'Keisha' is a person's name, not a place. The lodge mentioned likely refers to the Stowe lodge/gatehouse.
